creator: freebsd@nagilum.org
text:
Right, but it makes no difference.
To make the gcc48 stuff work I also have this in libmap.conf:
libgcc_s.so.1   gcc48/libgcc_s.so.1
libgomp.so.1    gcc48/libgomp.so.1
libobjc.so.3    gcc48/libobjc.so.4
libssp.so.0     gcc48/libssp.so.0
libstdc++.so.6  gcc48/libstdc++.so.6

Can you let me know where you find FAVORITE_COMPILER and  
GCC_DEFAULT_VERSION documented? I looked in make.conf(5) and  
/usr/share/examples/etc/make.conf and found nothing.
I tried your suggestions anyways but they made no difference.
Attached the log from the build using the standard compiler and  
options - again same problem I'm afraid.
I still find it more likely that the problem is that it attempts to  
link against the base openssl instead of the ports version. I believe  
the relevant part of the commandline would be:
"-L/usr/lib -lgssapi_krb5 -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib  -o dig  
${BASEOBJS} ${LIBS0} ../../lib/lwres/liblwres.a ../../lib/dns/libdns.a  
  -lgssapi_krb5 -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,-rpath,/usr/local/lib -lcrypto  
../../lib/bind9/libbind9.a ../../lib/isccfg/libisccfg.a   
../../lib/isc/libisc.a -L/usr/local/lib -lidnkit -L/usr/local/lib  
-R/usr/local/lib -liconv  -L/usr/local/lib -lxml2 -lz -L/usr/local/lib  
-liconv -lm"

..and indeed when I move the "-L/usr/lib" behind "-L/usr/local/lib"  
the problem goes away.

"-lgssapi_krb5 -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib  -o dig ${BASEOBJS} ${LIBS0}  
../../lib/lwres/liblwres.a ../../lib/dns/libdns.a  -lgssapi_krb5  
-L/usr/local/lib-Wl,-rpath,/usr/local/lib -lcrypto  
../../lib/bind9/libbind9.a ../../lib/isccfg/libisccfg.a   
../../lib/isc/libisc.a -L/usr/local/lib -L/usr/lib -lidnkit  
-L/usr/local/lib -R/usr/local/lib -liconv  -L/usr/local/lib -lxml2 -lz  
-L/usr/local/lib -liconv -lm"

So its just a matter of option ordering.
Thanks for looking into this so promptly!
Kind regards,
Alex.

Zitat von Mathieu Arnold <mat@mat.cc>:

> Ok.
>
> So, ok, let me begin that we don't support building with a non-default
> compiler.
>
> So, I tried with :
> make stage USE_GCC=4.8
> and it worked all right, like I figure it would.
> Then I tried :
> make stage CC=gcc48 CXX=g++48 CPP=cpp48
> And it failed, a bit like I expected it to fail. When you do that, you're
> missing a few other bits that should be there, like :
> CFLAGS=        -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc48
> CXXFLAGS=      -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc48
> LDFLAGS=       -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc48 -L/usr/local/lib/gcc48
>
> If you want ports to build with gcc 4.8 when it is said they need gcc, you
> want to put these in your make.conf :
> FAVORITE_COMPILER=gcc
> GCC_DEFAULT_VERSION=4.8
>
> But it may fail in some other ways, it's not supported, though, patch are
> welcome :-)
>
> --
> Mathieu Arnold
